response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of progressive full-stack experience with deep TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, Docker, Kubernetes, and AWS coverage that maps well to the primary stack. His current role at re:cinq explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which directly satisfies the increasingly important AI agent requirement. The main gaps are Nest.js (not mentioned anywhere), Vitest and Playwright (absent from listed skills), and meaningful Kotlin/Java work (only peripheral usage with robotic arms). PostgreSQL is also absent, though MySQL and MongoDB are present. Given the seniority level and breadth of systems experience, these gaps are learnable, making him a solid yes for interview with a skills-depth conversation around the missing stack specifics.
strengths: 15+ years of full-stack TypeScript/Node.js/React across product companies, low-latency systems, and enterprise tooling — well above the 6-year floor
Active AI agent and MCP framework development at current role, directly matching the job's AI agent experience requirement
Strong testing culture: TDD practitioner, built OSS testing libraries, taught TDD workshops — aligns with Vitest/Jest quality expectations even if Vitest itself isn't listed
concerns: Nest.js is entirely absent from the resume despite being the primary backend framework in the job's stack
Kotlin is missing and Java usage is peripheral (robotic arms, DevOps tooling) — secondary backend services in this role require meaningful JVM experience
Playwright and Vitest are not listed; testing breadth leans toward custom/DLang frameworks rather than the specified JS ecosystem tools
